soft_factors.name: internal_workflow_hard_filter
soft_factors.condition: Primary value proposition is improving internal processes, back-office efficiency, employee productivity, or in-house workflows
soft_factors.evidence: 16 rejections (appzen, haast, lyzr, obin-ai, pace, sycamore, galdera, meridian-ai, interloom, opencfo, numos, kaaj, harvey, lema-ai, nexus, octonomy); 0 upvotes across 16+ appearances

soft_factors.name: accounting_cfo_hard_filter
soft_factors.condition: Bookkeeping automation, accounts payable/receivable, CFO tools, expense management, small business finance operations
soft_factors.evidence: stacks 2★, basis 1★, opencfo 1★, numos 1★, kaaj 1★; 0 upvotes in 5 appearances

soft_factors.name: external_agent_governance
soft_factors.condition: Product governs, secures, authenticates, or monitors EXTERNAL/third-party agents in a marketplace or ecosystem (not internal institutional ops)
soft_factors.evidence: ralio 5★ ('works as long as external agents'), lema-ai 1★ ('looking for solutions that mitigate risk of third party agents'), openbox-ai 3★ ('could be interesting for third party agents rather than internal workflows'); 16 internal rejections confirm the inverse

soft_factors.name: agent_auth_security
soft_factors.condition: Agent identity standards, NHI management, zero-trust per-tool-call authorisation, agentic security against agent-specific attack vectors
soft_factors.evidence: t54-labs 5★, oasis-security 5★, keycard 5★ ('Spot on'), resistant-ai 5★ ('Novel and love the agentic security angle'), venice-security 4★, imper-ai 4★; 6/6 upvotes (100%) — strongest gap signal in corpus

soft_factors.name: dispute_resolution_agent_liability
soft_factors.condition: Agent risk evaluation, ADR certifications (SOC 2 for AI agents), liability coverage when AI agents cause harm, agent insurance
soft_factors.evidence: mount-insurance 5★ ('Reduces risk — excellent'), klaimee 4★ ('Not quite as good as Mount'); 2/2 upvotes (100%)

soft_factors.name: research_signal_intelligence_S1
soft_factors.condition: Alternative data / investment intelligence exposed as agent-callable API or agent, serving external agents or end investors (not internal research desks)
soft_factors.evidence: agentsmyth 5★ ('This is what we are looking for'), serafis 5★, kimpton-ai 5★; 3/3 upvotes (100%) — highest-conviction specialist agent category; highest single bonus in system

soft_factors.name: portfolio_integrated_tax
soft_factors.condition: Portfolio-integrated tax intelligence: TLH, tax-aware rebalancing, after-tax portfolio optimisation (NOT compliance/filing)
soft_factors.evidence: alomana 5★ (autonomous rebalancing + TLH); taxdown 2★ and juno 2★ confirm the boundary (compliance = bad, efficiencies = good)

soft_factors.name: agent_payment_rails
soft_factors.condition: Infrastructure enabling agents to initiate, authorise, and settle financial transactions with proper governance — escrow, intent verification, dispute resolution

soft_factors.name: ai_safety_guardrails_agent_specific
soft_factors.condition: Agent-level safety (not model-level): agentic behaviour monitoring, supervised agent operation in regulated contexts, compliance-as-code for agent governance
soft_factors.evidence: overmind 5★ (archived), manifold 5★, complyance 5★, kai 4★, norm-ai 4★; 5/5 upvotes when agent-specific; 3/3 downvotes when model-level (mindgard, goodfire, armorcode)

soft_factors.name: marketplace_platform_concept
soft_factors.condition: Agent marketplace infrastructure, curated agent ecosystems, platforms where multiple agents can be discovered and deployed
soft_factors.evidence: bank-of-bots 4★ ('Love this concept. Looks like something I'd like to play with'); 1 data point — emerging

soft_factors.name: compliance_differentiated
soft_factors.condition: Compliance companies that are architecturally differentiated, novel in approach, and finserv-specific (not generic execution or KYC/AML)
soft_factors.evidence: complyance 5★ (differentiated), norm-ai 4★ vs sphinx 3★ ('not exactly groundbreaking'), bretton-ai 3★ (wrong sub-domain)

soft_factors.name: tax_filing_compliance_penalty
soft_factors.condition: Primary product is tax filing, tax return preparation, tax compliance, CPA-style workflow (NOT tax strategies, TLH, or portfolio optimisation)
soft_factors.evidence: taxdown 2★ ('not interested in EU tax products or tax filing'), juno 2★ ('not tax compliance, tax efficiencies')

soft_factors.name: kyc_aml_penalty
soft_factors.condition: Primary positioning is financial crime compliance, AML screening, KYC verification, sanctions checking
soft_factors.evidence: bretton-ai 3★ ('not so interested in KYC and AML'), vivox-ai 2★ ('not interested in AML/KYC right now'); 0 upvotes in 2 explicit exposures

soft_factors.name: series_b_plus_penalty
soft_factors.condition: Series B or later, or large raise signalling the company is beyond seed/early-Series-A shaping stage
soft_factors.evidence: linx-security 2★ ('Just too late at series B'; score 94→2★ — most extreme near-miss in corpus), april 3★ ('Series B too late'), armadin 2★ ('raised too much'), basis 1★ ('raised too much')

soft_factors.name: ai_not_agent_level_penalty
soft_factors.condition: AI/LLM/model-level safety, interpretability, prompt injection, AI red-teaming — without specific agent governance framing
soft_factors.evidence: mindgard 2★ ('AI, not agents'), goodfire 2★ ($1.25B unicorn, no upvote), armorcode 2★ ('internal support, not breaking innovation'); 3/3 explicit AI-level rejections

soft_factors.name: non_finserv_penalty
soft_factors.condition: No finserv signal — targeting IT, DevOps, general enterprise, or other verticals exclusively
soft_factors.evidence: certivo 3★ ('Had this been for Finserv I would have been interested'), novee 1★ ('Not trying to solve problems for IT professionals')

soft_factors_pending.name: agent_exposure_api_callable
soft_factors_pending.condition: Company exposes intelligence/capability as an agent-callable API or MCP server (not dashboard-only)
soft_factors_pending.evidence: serafis 5★ ('this would be better if it exposed an agent'), kimpton-ai 5★ ('better if it exposed an agent'); 2/3 occurrences required for formal soft factor — ONE MORE VOTE NEEDED

changes.rationale: Gap category alone was necessary but not sufficient to predict vote outcome — 22 near-misses in a single cycle proved that gap_fit was over-weighted relative to its true predictive power. The Hard Filters (internal_workflow, accounting_cfo) now handle the dominant misprediction. Freed 5 points redistributed to stage_match.

Stage was under-weighted relative to its predictive power. Explicitly cited in 4+ rejections. Linx-Security (score 94, 2★ 'Just too late at series B') is the clearest example: a near-perfect gap_fit score was immediately overridden by stage. Stage should carry as much weight as finserv_readiness.

history.bootstrap_note: First calibration run (61 votes > 50 threshold). Soft factors established across 6 previous cycles were never serialised to this file. This entry bootstraps the full corpus of vote-evidenced soft factors: 2 hard filters, 9 positive bonuses (7 active + 2 emerging), 5 negative penalties. No new soft factors from this cycle's near-miss analysis (0 new near-misses). 1 pending soft factor: agent_exposure_api_callable at 2/3 evidence threshold — will formalise when third data point arrives.
